REFINED PALM OIL, EDIBLE MAIZE STARCH, COCOA SOLIDS (2:7% Per 1
MILK SOLIDS, RAISING AGENTS (500i)& 503(0)1, EMULSIRERS r
(330& 322(0)) AND IODISED SALT,
CONTAINS PERMITTED HATURAL (636&928)&SYNTHETIC

flagged_codes.correct_functional_class: emulsifier, stabilizer, anti-caking agent, glazing agent

ground_truth_status.notes: Verifiable INS codes found: 331, 500(i), 503(ii) (interpreting OCR '503(0)1' as 503(ii)), 330, 322(i), 636, 928, 470, 202, 476, 450. Codes 500(i) and 503(ii) are correctly used as raising agents. Codes 330 and 322(i) under emulsifiers are acceptable because citric acid can function as an emulsifying salt/acidity regulator in some contexts and lecithin is an emulsifier. INS 476 is not a food colour; it is an emulsifier, so if the text clearly assigns 476 to 'SYNTHETIC FOOD COLOURS' it would also be a mismatch, but the OCR is ambiguous because the phrase may refer jointly to colours and flavours. INS 450 under colours is also likely incorrect because it is a raising agent/emulsifier/stabilizer class, but due to OCR ambiguity and grouping syntax it is not flagged with certainty.

flagged_codes.context: [322(i) & 297], RAISING AGENTS [570 & 442], NATURE IDENTICAL AND ARTIFICIAL
ACIDITY REGULATORS (102 & 1101(i)), GELLING AGENT (440) AND COLOUR (554).

ground_truth_status.notes: Verified INS-coded additives parsed from the label: 322(i), 297, 570, 442, 102, 1101(i), 440, 554. Correct assignments found for 322(i) as emulsifier and 440 as gelling agent. INS 102 is Tartrazine (a colour), so its placement under ACIDITY REGULATORS is also a functional class mismatch; INS 1101(i) is protease and not an acidity regulator. However, because the label groups '102 & 1101(i)' together under ACIDITY REGULATORS and both are clearly INS-coded, both should be considered misassigned. Total mismatches therefore may be interpreted as 6 if grouped additives are counted individually.
